remedy_caveat: Most remedies mitigate, not cure; relocation is the ideal fix. Present remedies with this caveat.

entries.reasoning: Water element aligns N/NE; SE fire clash; SW earth clash (major hard-to-fix); Brahmasthan open.
entries.disagreement: NW allowed(VaastuMangaal) vs avoid(majority); W fine(VaastuMangaal) vs avoid(others); never on NE-SW diagonal.
entries.remedy: Relocate to N/NE or fill+close. SW stuck: paint top yellow (never blue/black/green/brown). Mitigation: copper strips, pyramid, rock salt. No leakage; covered; septic away.

entries.remedy: Shift to SW/W. NE unavoidable: keep small/light, copper/lead strips, add SW counter-mass. Never above kitchen/pooja.

entries.reasoning: Active groundwater = water element; NE Ishanya prosperity; SE fire, SW stability clash; never on Brahmasutra; E/N open for sun.

entries.remedy: Wrong/dead bore: fill with earth, close, lockable covered slab. Can't close: cover+buffer. Dig before construction; 15-20ft from wall; not touching house walls; away from septic; round shape.

entries.remedy: Fill/close wrong or dry well; lockable covered slab if cannot close; boundary buffer between house and well.

entries.reasoning: Primary energy inlet; NE morning sun; N Kubera wealth; E sun first rays.

Good padas: N3/N4/N5, E3/E4. Bad: E1/E2 Shikhi/Parjanya, NE Shikhi. Engine present 2 layers (beginner direction + 32-pada advanced).
entries.remedy: Avoid SW-facing. Mitigation: brass wire/metal strips at threshold; warm colours (green/blue/brown, no black); Om/Swastika/Ganesh/Lakshmi; plants; toran; partition. Generic mirror/wind-chime don't fix structural pada defects.

entries.reasoning: Blocks negative energy; energetic boundary; retains wealth.

entries.remedy: Dwar vedha: toran, partition/curtain/jali screen, keep toilet door shut. Mirror-facing-door: relocate. Creak: oil hinges. Replace broken doors.

entries.disagreement: Fridge SW vs W vs S; firm rule = not NE.
entries.remedy: Move fridge out of NE to SW/W; grain storage NE->S/W.
entries.additional_detail_2026_recheck: Cross-checked against 6 more sources (2026): SW remains the dominant answer (5 of 6 new sources), with W, S, NW as accepted fallbacks — matches this KB's original verdict. Door should open toward East or North. Avoid dark-coloured fridges (black/deep blue); white, cream, or light blue preferred. Keep a few inches from walls and away from the stove (fire-water clash, same logic as KI-04 sink).
entries.additional_disagreement_2026_recheck: One outlier source (Bejandaruwalla) claims NW is 'always auspicious' as the primary direction rather than a fallback — this contradicts the 5-source majority favoring SW-first, and is noted as a minority view, not adopted as the primary verdict.
entries.gap_check_status: RECHECKED against question-database claim (NW-primary) — original SW-primary verdict CONFIRMED by 5/6 new sources, discrepancy resolved, not changed.

entries.reasoning: Mirrors=energy amplifiers multiply reflection; N wealth, E clarity; reflecting bed=sleep/health/quarrels; reflecting door=energy repelled.

entries.disagreement: Room zone NE vs E/W-of-house-N-second (Astrotalk internal). Facing uncontested: E first N second avoid S/NW.
entries.remedy: Rotate desk to E/N; remove mirror; 3-4in wall gap; Saraswati/Ganesha image + crystal globe.

entries.reasoning: TV electronic/fire-linked SE; glass reflects like mirror (mirror-facing-bed caution).

entries.reasoning: Complements SD-05; solid headboard grounding.

entries.disagreement: SW: earthy(consensus) vs blue(SMR outlier). NE: light-blue vs white/cream(both light). E: white/light-blue vs green/brown(both light). Universal avoid: black/dark absorbs, grey dullness, red+blue clash.
entries.remedy: Repaint to zone shade or use accents/furnishings/curtains; no structural fix; easiest domain to change.

entries.reasoning: Disposal function -> disposal zones (NW, SW-adjacent), never sacred NE; seat on N-S axis.

entries.remedy: Door closed; exhaust E/N; sea salt bowl inside; Vastu pyramid outer wall; money/spider/palm plant inside; light colours; slope drainage N/E/NE; commode raised 1-2ft; NE->Yantra/crystals/box; SE->wood+candle image; never share wall with kitchen/pooja.

entries.reasoning: Heavy + up-down movement -> heavy SW/S/W; NE light/open; clockwise = positive flow.
entries.disagreement: Step-count odd-preferred consensus but echoes doors/steps modern-view-count-doesnt-matter split. Minor.
entries.remedy: NE staircase: keep light, add lighting, light finish; symbolic crystals; under-stair clutter-free, no pooja/kitchen; clockwise; good ventilation; no broken treads.

entries.disagreement: See tulsi/money sub-entries.
entries.remedy: Cactus/thorny -> outdoor SW; remove bonsai/dead/artificial; earthen pots; trim dead leaves; odd counts.
entries.bamboo_addendum: Bamboo plant (lucky bamboo): considered auspicious in both Vastu and Feng Shui, low-maintenance, can be placed almost anywhere indoors including bedroom (unlike cactus/thorny plants) — bedroom-safe exception noted across sources (Livspace, Astrotalk).

entries.disagreement: Best dir NE(Astraroot/UltraTech/Housivity) vs East-ideal(JSWOneHomes/plantsinformation) vs N/NE/E-all-fine(most). NE/E/N all auspicious, NE most-cited.
entries.remedy: Relocate to NE/E raised platform; earthen pot; keep healthy; away from toilet/stairs.

entries.note: SE=wealth(Venus/Ganesha), N=Kubera/career; SE+N opposite-corners balance; clockwise growth; keep bushy.

entries.remedy: Relocate to SE or N; clockwise growth; away from red; healthy/bushy.
Relocate to N/E side wall; if fixed angle/cover; Bagua mirror is different protective use (small, outside door).

entries.reasoning: Vehicles = movement/Vayu -> NW; SE acceptable.
entries.disagreement: Dedicated parking-vastu sources thin this pass; documented at lower confidence per instruction; optional future top-up.
entries.remedy: Shift heavy vehicle out of NE to NW/SE; keep NE light/open.

entries.note: Foundation for all room placement reasoning.

entries.reasoning: Room element matches zone: fire-rooms SE, heavy/rest SW, sacred/light NE, disposal NW/SW-adjacent, wealth N; Brahmasthan open.
entries.disagreement: Children W-vs-NW; guest NW-vs-E/SE; study W-vs-NE-vs-E; center-pooja minority(AppliedVastu) vs keep-open(most). Main-door/toilet/staircase carry own pada/16-zone splits (DE-01,MI-01,MI-02).
entries.remedy: Change room-usage not walls (NE bedroom->pooja; SW room->master); pyramids/yantras; crystals in SW; sea-salt NE toilet; partition incompatible zones; colour/lighting; plants; extend deficient corners; keep Brahmasthan clear.

rules: SE=Agni: kitchen/fire, not UG-water not pooja
SW=earth/heavy: master bed+heaviest furniture, never empty, not kitchen/water/toilet-pit
entries.note: Engine should surface these prominently; violations = major dosh, near-universal consensus.

Sub-project beneath Cat1 room-zone layer. Room-zone matrix (RZ-01) answers practical search queries; pada layer adds per-cell expert precision (cf main-door DE-01), build incrementally post-launch. NOT one of the 10 top-level categories.

entries.reasoning: Waste/disposal function parallels toilet logic (MI-01) -> NW most-cited; exact SW corner (stability zone) and NE (sacred zone) both avoided; keep distance from wells/borewells and main living spaces.
entries.disagreement: NW vs 'south-of-southwest-but-not-exact-corner' vs SE-with-remedy — three different beginner rules exist; NW is the most-repeated single answer, so ranked first. Distance from house corners cited differently (2ft above ground vs plinth-level rules) across sources.
entries.remedy: If already wrong-placed: copper strip or copper sheet on the tank lid; plant a neem tree nearby; light-blue paint on the tank cover; keep at least a few feet from wells, main entrance, and bedrooms; ensure no leaks (practical hygiene matters more than direction).

entries.best_note: not a direction rule — a defect-correction topic for irregular/L-shaped plots
entries.principle: NE corner missing/cut = most serious defect (blocks spiritual/health energy); SW or S cut = comparatively easier to manage. NE corner EXTENDED (plot juts outward) is considered POSITIVE, up to ~10% of total area. SW extension is considered the single most harmful extension of any direction.
entries.reasoning: Vastu Purusha's body must be 'complete' (square/rectangular) for balanced energy; a missing corner is read as a missing body part of the deity pinned at that point, an extension as an amplification of that zone's quality.
entries.disagreement: No single formula works for irregular plots — several sources explicitly warn that 'this shape is good/bad' one-line answers are inadequate and a full plot-specific audit is needed. Treat this entry as general-pattern guidance, not a definitive per-plot verdict.
entries.remedy: Missing NE corner: mirrors on the two flanking walls facing the cut (to 'energetically extend' it), a Vastu pyramid at the outer corner point, a tulsi plant near the point, keep the area very clean and well-lit. Missing/extended SW: partition strip or physical wall to separate the irregular area from the main living zone; heavy earthy objects (rocks, yellow items) to ground excess air (for NW-type cuts) or crystals/citrine for SW. None of these remedies match a truly geometric corner — they reduce impact, they don't eliminate it. For serious defects (large NE cut, large SW extension) sources recommend a plot-specific professional audit rather than generic fixes.
entries.gap_check_status: Added from vastu_question_database.md cross-reference gap check (2026-07)

entries.reasoning: Aquarium = moving water = water element -> NE/N/E (water/wealth zones); South clashes as it's linked to lower income/high-expense claims; center-of-room placement lacks structural support and blocks movement.
entries.disagreement: Most sources say NE/N/E for wealth; one source (Quora/Shiva Om) recommends ESE specifically for the 'churning' quality of the aquarium; another (TrueVastu) says SW is ideal for the LIVING ROOM specifically 'to boost social skills,' contradicting the general avoid-SW rule — flagged as a room-specific exception, not the general answer.
entries.remedy: Relocate to NE/N/E of living room, against a solid wall, not center of room. Keep water clean and moving (a stagnant/dirty tank is considered worse than any direction mistake). Odd number of fish (commonly 9: 8 gold + 1 black) is a popular but non-universal folk addition. Avoid direct sunlight (practical: algae growth) and avoid the kitchen entirely.
entries.gap_check_status: Added from vastu_question_database.md cross-reference gap check (2026-07)

entries.reasoning: Since a tenant cannot move walls, doors, or plumbing, sources consistently redirect rented-home guidance toward furniture placement, colours, lighting, plants, and small symbolic remedies rather than structural fixes.
entries.disagreement: One traditional source (VaastuInternational) makes a distinct claim: Vastu defects in a rented house affect the OWNER more than the tenant (since it's the owner's asset), and that religious/pooja benefits performed by a tenant primarily accrue to the owner. This is a minority, more classical/technical claim not repeated by the mainstream modern sources, which mostly just say 'apply what you can, non-structurally.' Presented as a genuine disagreement, not a consensus fact.
entries.remedy: Renter-friendly fixes only: money plant in N, mirror in N (not facing bed), salt-water bowl in NE (change weekly), warm lighting in SE, rose quartz/amethyst in SW, wind chimes in NW, correct sleeping direction (free, high-impact), toran/pyramid/Om symbol at the entrance if the door direction can't be changed.
entries.gap_check_status: Added from vastu_question_database.md cross-reference gap check (2026-07)

comparison.origin: Vastu = India (Sanskrit, 5000+ yrs); Feng Shui = China
comparison.elements: Vastu: earth, water, fire, air, space. Feng Shui: wood, fire, earth, metal, water.
comparison.focus: Vastu = structural (direction of doors/rooms/whole-building orientation, best applied at construction stage). Feng Shui = non-structural (furniture arrangement, colour, decluttering, mostly applicable to any existing space).
comparison.best_use_case: New construction/major renovation -> Vastu is more practical (shapes the foundation). Existing/rented home -> Feng Shui's flexible, non-structural approach is easier to apply.

entries.disagreement: None significant — sources broadly agree on this origin/focus/use-case framing.
entries.remedy: Not applicable — this is a comparison/explainer entry, not a placement rule. Practical guidance from sources: use Vastu at the building/construction stage if possible, and layer Feng-Shui-style non-structural adjustments (declutter, furniture, colour) on top of whatever the fixed structure already is.

toolkit.sea_salt: Small bowl of uncrushed sea salt in problem corners; replace every 2-4 weeks (frequency varies by source, roughly bi-weekly to monthly); absorbs stagnant/negative energy. Can also mix in floor-cleaning water.
toolkit.camphor_crystals: Two camphor balls/crystals placed where things feel 'stuck' (career, finance); replace once fully shriveled.
toolkit.wind_chimes: 6 or 8 hollow-rod metal chimes for NW/W (metal/air zones); wooden chimes for E/SE (wood/fire zones); at entrances or problem corners.
toolkit.pyramids_yantras: Small metal/crystal pyramids at the entrance or the specific defect corner; material (lead/brass/zinc/crystal) is matched to the direction/defect by a consultant in serious cases.
toolkit.crystals: Clear quartz balls or direction-specific stones (rose quartz/amethyst for SW-relationships, citrine for SW-earth) — color/type tied to the intention.
toolkit.mirrors: Used to 'energetically extend' a missing corner (see MI-06), or reposition negative reflections (see FF-03) — not a generic cure-all, direction-specific.
toolkit.color: Repainting or adding a zone-matched accent (cushion, curtain, artwork) is one of the lowest-cost, most-repeated remedies across the entire KB (see CO-01/CO-02).

entries.reasoning: These are the standard 'no-demolition' correction tools cited across nearly every remedy entry in this KB — consolidated here as a single reference so the engine doesn't need to repeat the same list inside every individual remedy.
entries.disagreement: Salt-replacement frequency varies (weekly to monthly) across sources — no consensus on exact interval. Some sources treat these as near-magical fixes ('changes your life'); this KB frames them consistently as mitigation tools, not cures — see remedy_caveat at the top of this file.
entries.remedy: This IS the remedy-reference entry — cross-linked from other entries' 'remedy' fields rather than repeating the full list every time. Always mitigation-only; structural correction remains the 'ideal fix' per this KB's global caveat.
query_aliases: sea salt wind chime camphor vastu remedy list

entries.reasoning: A single mattress is read as symbolizing togetherness/unity for a couple; two joined mattresses (common when combining two singles into a 'king') are said to symbolically create a 'crack' or division between partners.
entries.disagreement: Only one strong source found explicitly on this specific point — flag as lower-confidence, single-source claim rather than broad consensus, unlike most other entries in this KB.
entries.remedy: If replacing the mattress isn't practical, sources suggest at minimum using one continuous top sheet/mattress topper across both mattresses to visually and physically 'join' the sleeping surface.

entries.placement_note: East/North/Southwest walls generally preferred for bedroom art; avoid south wall and floor-level placement for deity images specifically.
entries.reasoning: Imagery is read as a constant subconscious cue — paired/harmonious images reinforce togetherness, solitary/violent/sad images are read as reinforcing the corresponding negative state.
entries.disagreement: None significant — broad agreement on avoid-list (violence, sadness, solitary figures, storms) and recommend-list (pairs, nature, romance) across all sources checked.
entries.remedy: Simply swap the artwork — this is one of the lowest-cost, lowest-effort remedies in the entire KB, no direction-measurement needed, just subject-matter and general wall zone.

entries.summary: The Vastu Purusha Mandala is the foundational cosmic diagram underlying ALL Vastu room-placement rules. A mythological being (Vastu Purusha) is depicted pinned face-down on a grid, head toward NE, feet toward SW. The grid is divided into 81 squares (9x9, the 'Paramashayika' mandala used for homes), governed by 45 deities (32 outer + 13 inner, hence '45-pada'). Every room-placement rule (kitchen->SE, pooja->NE, master bed->SW, etc.) is derived from which deity's 'body part' occupies that square, and what protecting/honoring that deity is believed to support.
entries.grid_sizes: Classical texts describe multiple mandala sizes (1, 4, 9, 16, 25, 64, 81-pada) for different building types; 81-pada (9x9) is standard for residential homes — this is the basis of this KB's '45-pada' room-zone layer (RZ-PADA, 3/45 done).
entries.reasoning: This is explanatory/mythological content, not an actionable placement instruction — included so users can understand WHY room-placement rules exist, and so it can be linked from every room-placement entry as background.
entries.disagreement: Minor variations in the origin myth's specific details (which god fought which demon) across retellings — the structural facts (81 squares, 45 deities, head-NE/feet-SW orientation) are consistent across all sources.
entries.remedy: Not applicable — explainer entry. Cross-reference from RZ-PADA and any individual pada entry.
entries.gap_check_status: Added from vastu_question_database.md cross-reference gap check (2026-07)

entries.reasoning: A fully vacant/enclosed space below the house is generally considered inauspicious in classical Vastu; a basement is treated as an exception if it stays partially above grade (ventilation/light) and isn't used as a bedroom.
entries.disagreement: Only one strong source found with this level of detail — this is a thinner-sourced entry than most in the KB; treat as directional guidance, not high-consensus fact.
entries.remedy: If already built with a basement not matching these conditions: use it for storage/utility only, keep well-lit and light-colored, avoid using as a bedroom or main living space.
entries.gap_check_status: Added from vastu_question_database.md cross-reference gap check (2026-07)

entries.ratio_note: If not a perfect square, sources agree rectangular is next-best; one source specifically recommends the longer sides run North-South rather than East-West; a plot narrower at the entrance and wider at the rear is considered good, the reverse is not. No single numeric ratio limit (e.g. exact 1:2) was found consistently across sources — this KB does not invent a specific number.
entries.reasoning: Regular/symmetric shapes are read as matching the Vastu Purusha's 'complete body' (see RZ-03); irregular shapes create 'missing' or 'extended' zones (see MI-06).
entries.disagreement: One source (HuntProperty) says hexagon (8 even sides) is also acceptable, while others only endorse square/rectangle — flagged as a minority allowance.
entries.remedy: For already-irregular plots, see MI-06 (missing/extended corner remedies) — compound-wall shaping to create a visually square/rectangular boundary is the most-cited practical fix.
query_aliases: ghar ka shape kaisa hona chahiye
entries.gap_check_status: Added from vastu_question_database.md cross-reference gap check (2026-07)

entries.reasoning: Living room is a high-traffic, guest-facing, energy-receiving space -> placed in the same auspicious open-energy zones as the main entrance (NE/N/E), consistent with RZ-01's room-zone matrix. This is the dedicated entry for what was previously only present inside the RZ-01 summary matrix (a 🔴 high-frequency question in the cross-reference database that lacked its own confidence/disagreement/remedy breakdown).
entries.disagreement: None significant on the room-zone itself; note that heavy seating/sofa placement WITHIN the living room follows the separate S/W-wall-heavy-furniture rule (FF-06) — the room's overall zone (NE/N/E) and the sofa's specific wall (S/W) are two different, non-contradicting rules operating at different scales.
entries.remedy: If living room is already in a less-ideal zone, focus remedies on furniture placement (FF-06) and colour (CO-02) within the room rather than relocating the room itself.
entries.gap_check_status: Added from vastu_question_database.md cross-reference gap check (2026-07)

entries.reasoning: One source notes the kitchen should be directly above/below another kitchen (same vertical stack) rather than below a bedroom, on the logic that fire-element rooms shouldn't stack under rest-element rooms across floors.
entries.disagreement: Only one source found addressing this specific multi-floor stacking question — this KB flags it as thin/low-confidence rather than asserting it as established consensus, consistent with 'never invent, mark honestly.'
entries.remedy: If a kitchen already sits below/above a bedroom in an existing multi-floor home: no structural remedy is commonly cited beyond the general kitchen colour/stove-direction remedies (KI-01–KI-05); treat as low-priority.

gap_check.source: vastu_question_database.md (~65 questions cross-referenced against internet Vastu sources)
gap_check.result: 23/24 high-frequency (red) items were already covered as dedicated entries; 1 (living room) was promoted from the RZ-01 matrix-only mention to its own dedicated entry (RZ-06). 1 discrepancy found and resolved (fridge direction: KI-05's SW-primary reconfirmed against question-db's NW claim via 6 new sources). 13 new dedicated entries added covering yellow/white gaps, plus 1 addendum (bamboo, folded into existing MI-03 plants entry since it is a minor variant of an already-covered topic).
gap_check.not_added_honest_gap: astrology_vs_vastu_override — searched but found no sufficiently sourced content to write a proper entry; left out rather than invented, per this KB's own rule.
